channel.title: Frontion - Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia arrests six opposition candidates day before election
item.description: Armenian authorities detained six candidates from a pro-Russian opposition party on Saturday, one day before parliamentary elections seen as a test of the government's pivot away from Moscow toward the West. Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: ⛪ Over one million gather in Madrid for Pope Leo XIV Mass
item.description: More than a million people filled Madrid streets and Plaza de Cibeles for Pope Leo XIV's main open-air Mass during his first papal visit to Spain, in what officials called the largest event of his week-long trip. Source: Reuters | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenians vote under Russian pressure to prevent Western drift
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan faces opposition from pro-Russian parties in a parliamentary election that tests Armenia's efforts to forge peace with Azerbaijan and move away from Moscow's orbit after its 2023 military defeat. Source: NBC News | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan delivers special letter to Iran's supreme leader
item.description: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi traveled to Tehran with messages from the prime minister and army chief as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the US-Iran conflict and prevent further escalation in the Gulf. Source: Al Jazeera | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greek police arrest Palestinian man suspected of Hamas attack plot
item.description: Police on the Greek island of Crete arrested a 37-year-old Palestinian man on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and belonging to the militant group Hamas, authorities said. Source: AP News | Category: Europe

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 With China's Xi in North Korea, Kim projects confidence and defiance
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un welcomes Chinese President Xi Jinping to Pyongyang on Monday, feeling in a position of strength with a firm ally in Russia, a nuclear arsenal and little apparent appetite to engage with Washington. Source: Reuters | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia votes in test of PM's pivot away from Russia
item.description: Armenians head to the polls in a parliamentary election seen as a test of the government's efforts to forge a peace deal after a crushing military defeat by Azerbaijan three years ago. Source: France 24 | Category: Europe

item.title: 💰 ECB interest rate hikes begin as inflation pressures mount
item.description: The European Central Bank begins interest rate increases as economic pressures and inflation concerns drive monetary policy shifts across the eurozone. Source: LiveMint | Category: Economy

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore blocks anti-Indian content, cites China origin
item.description: Singapore authorities invoked the Online Criminal Harms Act to restrict access to posts targeting the Indian community, saying the content likely originated from China. Source: Firstpost | Category: Asia-Pacific

item.title: 🏛️ Trump faces new Republican resistance as midterm pressures build
item.description: Republicans and Democrats are skeptical that Trump faces an actual revolt, but a greater willingness to break with him could pose a threat to his most ambitious initiatives between now and Election Day. Source: Reuters | Category: US Politics

item.title: 🛢️ Strait of Hormuz closure affects 1 billion lives, UN warns
item.description: A United Nations official warned that closure of the Strait of Hormuz would affect over a billion people globally, highlighting the critical nature of the waterway for global energy supplies. Source: Khaleej Times | Category: Energy

item.title: 🤖 OpenAI plans ChatGPT 'superapp' overhaul ahead of listing
item.description: OpenAI is planning its biggest ChatGPT overhaul yet, aiming to turn it into a "superapp" with coding tools and AI agents to boost revenue ahead of a potential stock market listing, the Financial Times reported. Source: Reuters | Category: Technology

item.title: ✈️ Airbus closing in on widebody order from Scandinavian airline
item.description: Airbus is closing in on a widebody order from Scandinavia's biggest airline SAS AB, which includes a mix of the Airbus A330neo and the more advanced A350, with about 15 to 20 aircraft under consideration. Source: Reuters | Category: Defense Industry

item.title: ✈️ Boeing can provide aftermarket support for China's 200-jet order
item.description: A Boeing executive said the company can provide aftermarket support for China's 200-jet order despite geopolitical tensions affecting the aerospace industry. Source: Reuters | Category: Defense Industry

item.title: 💸 High fuel costs trigger airline failures and consolidation
item.description: Global airline chiefs at their annual summit in Rio de Janeiro say budget carriers have been among the hardest hit by higher fuel costs, with industry failures and consolidation expected to continue. Source: Reuters | Category: Economy

item.title: 📊 ECB raises rates as inflation pressures mount
item.description: The European Central Bank has raised interest rates as economic pressures and inflation concerns drive monetary policy shifts across the eurozone. Source: RTÉ | Category: Economy

item.title: 🛢️ Domestic LPG prices soar as energy costs climb
item.description: Domestic LPG cylinder prices have risen significantly, with a cylinder in Delhi now costing 942 rupees amid broader energy price increases. Source: Energy Economic Times | Category: Energy

item.title: 🇺🇸 US eyes Iranian assets for Gulf allies' reconstruction
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has directed a team to assess costs for damage already inflicted on Gulf allies by Iran, as Washington considers redirecting frozen Iranian assets for reconstruction efforts. Source: Reuters | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli strike in Gaza kills seven, including two women
item.description: An Israeli strike killed at least seven Palestinians including two women in Gaza on Saturday, health officials said, as mediators restarted talks in Cairo with Hamas and other factions over safeguarding a strained ceasefire agreement. Source: Reuters | Category: Middle East

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia opens door to 'new relationship' with US as Putin wrestles with Ukraine deadlock
item.description: Talks appear no closer to ending the war, but Russian President Vladimir Putin has sought to project a bullish view of the conflict while signaling openness to engagement with Washington. Source: NBC News | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇷🇺🇹🇷 Russia-Turkey dynamics shift as Putin and Erdogan navigate Ukraine
item.description: The evolving relationship between Moscow and Ankara reflects broader geopolitical shifts as both leaders manage competing interests in the Ukraine conflict. Source: New York Times | Category: Diplomacy

item.title: 🇺🇸 US says it destroyed two Iranian drones targeting Hormuz shipping
item.description: The U.S. military said it shot down two Iranian attack drones threatening Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic, the latest clash between warring forces as tensions escalate in the Gulf region. Source: Al Arabiya | Category: US-Iran War

item.title: 🇺🇦 Railway infrastructure struck by Ukrainian drones in occupied territories
item.description: Ukrainian forces carried out drone strikes on railway infrastructure in the occupied Luhansk and Donetsk regions, targeting logistics lines used by Russian forces. Source: Kyiv Independent | Category: Ukraine-Russia War

item.title: 🇵🇰 Arrests in Pakistan-administered Kashmir amid political tensions
item.description: Authorities arrested supporters of a banned group in Pakistan-administered Kashmir amid ongoing political tensions in the region. Source: Nikkei Asia | Category: South Asia

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea vows to keep nuclear status as Xi prepares rare Pyongyang visit
item.description: Kim Yo Jong, the sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, said North Korea will never renounce its status as a nuclear weapons state ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang. Source: NV | Category: Asia-Pacific
